obj.content: Which [b] TWO [/b] benefits for children of learning to write did both students find surprising?
obj.html_content: Which TWO benefits for children of learning to write did both students find surprising?

obj.content: improved fine motor skills
obj.html_content: improved fine motor skills

obj.content: improved memory
obj.html_content: improved memory

obj.content: improved concentration
obj.html_content: improved concentration

obj.content: improved imagination
obj.html_content: improved imagination

obj.content: improved spatial awareness
obj.html_content: improved spatial awareness

sentence.content: MAN: How are you getting on with the assignment on handwriting?
sentence.content_zh: 男：你那篇关于书写能力的作业做得怎么样了？

sentence.content: WOMAN: Not too bad. You know, I hadn’t realised that children benefit in so many ways from learning to write.
sentence.content_zh: 女：还不错。你知道吗，我以前不知道孩子们从学习书写中能获得这么多好处。

sentence.content: WOMAN: It’s such an important skill and yet most people think handwriting is less important than in the past because people hardly ever write by hand these days.
sentence.content_zh: 女：这是一项非常重要的技能，但大多数人认为现在书写已经没有以前那么重要了，因为现在几乎没人用手写字了。

sentence.content: MAN: Yes. And all the evidence suggests children should learn to write by hand before they learn to type. Not least because it helps their memory.
sentence.content_zh: 男：是的。而且所有证据都表明，孩子应该先学会手写，再学打字。其中一个主要原因就是这对他们的记忆力有帮助。

sentence.content: WOMAN: That’s right. The physical act of writing helps children to remember letters. That seems pretty obvious when you think about it.
sentence.content_zh: 女：没错。动手写字这个行为本身就能帮助孩子记住字母的形状。仔细想想其实挺明显的。

sentence.content: MAN: What’s less obvious is how it helps develop their concentration. They have to sit still and focus on one thing.
sentence.content_zh: 男：不太明显的是它如何帮助他们发展注意力。他们必须坐好，专注于一件事上。

sentence.content: WOMAN: Yeah. That aspect of handwriting had never occurred to me before.
sentence.content_zh: 女：对。这方面我以前真的没怎么想过。

sentence.content: MAN: Same here. I’m not sure I understand how it improves children’s imagination though.
sentence.content_zh: 男：我也是。不过我不太明白它是怎么提升孩子想象力的。

sentence.content: WOMAN: Well, there was that study which showed that primary aged children generated more ideas when they were writing by hand than using a keyboard. I would’ve guessed that would be the case.
sentence.content_zh: 女：嗯，有一项研究显示，小学生在手写时比用键盘能产生更多创意点子。我觉得这也很合理。

sentence.content: MAN: Mmm, yeah. I’d never associated spatial awareness with handwriting either. I thought spatial awareness was more to do with knowing where you are in relation to objects or other people.
sentence.content_zh: 男：嗯，是的。我也从来没把空间意识和手写联系在一起过。我一直以为空间意识是指你对自己在物体或他人周围位置的判断。

sentence.content: WOMAN: I thought that too. But good spatial awareness is essential for writing because you have to space words correctly.
sentence.content_zh: 女：我以前也这么想。但良好的空间意识对手写来说其实非常重要，因为你得正确地安排单词之间的间距。

sentence.content: WOMAN: It’s not just fine motor skills that improve through writing as I’d always assumed.
sentence.content_zh: 女：原来我以为只是精细动作技能会通过书写得到提高。

sentence.content: MAN: Handwriting is so much harder for children with dyspraxia, who have problems coordinating movement.
sentence.content_zh: 男：对于患有动作协调障碍的孩子来说，书写要困难得多，因为他们很难控制身体动作。

sentence.content: MAN: It’s good there are lots of things you can do in the classroom to help them.
sentence.content_zh: 男：幸好有很多方法可以在教室里帮助他们。

sentence.content: MAN: They need so much more support with letter formation – you need to play lots of games to help them distinguish letter shapes. It takes a lot of patience.
sentence.content_zh: 男：他们在形成字母方面需要更多的支持——你需要玩很多游戏来帮助他们区分不同字母的形状。这很考验耐心。

sentence.content: WOMAN: Yeah. I liked the idea of using one of those pens that lights up if you press too hard. That seems like a really simple solution.
sentence.content_zh: 女：嗯。我喜欢那个如果用力太大会亮灯的笔的设计，看起来是个很简单有效的办法。

sentence.content: MAN: Yes, absolutely. I’m not sure there’s much you can do about children with dyspraxia writing very slowly.
sentence.content_zh: 男：是的，确实如此。不过我也不确定有没有办法让患有动作协调障碍的孩子写得更快一些。

sentence.content: MAN: It’s more important to focus on accuracy and as they get more confident I think they eventually speed up.
sentence.content_zh: 男：更重要的是准确性，随着他们越来越自信，速度也会慢慢提上来。

sentence.content: WOMAN: One quite simple thing you can do is to use grid paper. So they write each letter in a box and that trains them to space the letters correctly.
sentence.content_zh: 女：有个比较简单的方法是使用格子纸，每个字母写在一个格子里，这样可以训练他们正确安排字母间距。

sentence.content: MAN: Indeed, that’s more important for legibility than trying to get them to write in a straight line.
sentence.content_zh: 男：没错，比起让他们写出一条直线，清晰的字母间距对辨识度更为重要。

sentence.content: MAN: For some children it might be better to teach them to write on a laptop rather than by hand. Like children with dyslexia.
sentence.content_zh: 男：有些孩子可能更适合教他们在笔记本电脑上打字而不是手写，比如像患有诵读困难症的孩子。

sentence.content: MAN: They often really struggle with handwriting and some just give up.
sentence.content_zh: 男：他们经常在书写方面遇到很大困难，有些人甚至直接放弃了。

sentence.content: WOMAN: Yeah. It’s not as frustrating for them if they get things wrong – on a keyboard they can be more willing to have a go. But I read that developing fluency isn’t any faster.
sentence.content_zh: 女：是的。对他们来说，写错也没关系，用键盘更容易让他们愿意尝试。但我读到说，这样在提高写作流利度方面并没有更快。

sentence.content: MAN: That’s right. Did you read that article on the benefits of teaching print rather than cursive handwriting, where the letters are joined up?
sentence.content_zh: 男：没错。你有没有看过那篇关于教印刷体而非连笔草书的好处的文章？

sentence.content: WOMAN: Yes. Well in the past cursive writing was certainly considered more stylish and educated.
sentence.content_zh: 女：看了。过去人们普遍认为连笔书法更优雅、更有文化感。

sentence.content: WOMAN: But not anymore. Teachers’ attitudes have changed because it’s been proved that cursive is more difficult to learn – especially for children with learning difficulties who find joining up letters really challenging.
sentence.content_zh: 女：但现在不是了。教师们的看法已经改变，因为事实证明连笔更难学——特别是对那些学习困难的孩子，他们觉得把字母连起来特别难。

sentence.content: MAN: I agree. I was always worried that my poor handwriting affected my exam results. And now research shows that I was right to worry.
sentence.content_zh: 男：我同意这点。我一直担心我的糟糕字迹会影响考试成绩，现在研究证明我当时担心是有道理的。

sentence.content: MAN: I’m sure a lot of students think it’s unfair that they’re being judged on their handwriting not just their knowledge.
sentence.content_zh: 男：我相信很多学生会觉得不公平，因为评判他们不仅看知识掌握程度，还要看字写得好不好。

sentence.content: WOMAN: Marks are definitely affected if examiners can’t read the script. That’s why it’s always been so important to teach children to write legibly….
sentence.content_zh: 女：如果阅卷老师看不懂字迹，分数肯定会受影响。这就是为什么一直强调教会孩子写清楚的重要性……

sentence.content: WOMAN: Do you think the role of handwriting will change in the future?
sentence.content_zh: 女：你觉得将来书写的角色会改变吗？

sentence.content: MAN: I can’t see that changing much. Touch typing still isn’t taught in most schools, which is a shame. But maybe that won’t be necessary in the future because people will also be able to write by hand on digital devices.
sentence.content_zh: 男：我看不会有多大变化。毕竟大多数学校仍然不教盲打，这挺可惜的。但也许未来这并不重要，因为人们也可以在数字设备上用手写输入了。

sentence.content: MAN: Anyway, teachers understand the value of handwriting. It’s a basic life skill.
sentence.content_zh: 男：不管怎么说，老师们还是知道书写的价值的。它是一种基本的生活技能。

sentence.content: WOMAN: True. However, the fact is that people are writing by hand less and less and relying on digital devices. That does cause some problems.
sentence.content_zh: 女：没错。但现实是，人们越来越少用手写字，而是依赖数码设备。这也带来了一些问题。

sentence.content: MAN: You mean like note taking? There are lots of apps for that.
sentence.content_zh: 男：你是说像记笔记这种事吗？这类应用现在已经有很多了。

sentence.content: WOMAN: And for reading historical documents apparently. But my mum is shocked by my awful spelling and the fact that my punctuation is really inconsistent. I think you can put that down to lack of practice.
sentence.content_zh: 女：还有阅读历史文献的时候据说也有问题。但我妈妈总抱怨我拼写差得离谱，标点也非常混乱。我觉得这是因为缺乏练习。

sentence.content: MAN: I expect so. Personally, I miss writing by hand. I hardly ever write anything now. I remember my grandparents had such beautiful handwriting and it was so individual.
sentence.content_zh: 男：应该是这样的。我自己倒是怀念写字的感觉。我现在几乎什么都不写了。我还记得我祖父母那优美的字迹，每个人都有自己的风格。

sentence.content: MAN: Nobody I know would be able to identify my handwriting now. It’s a shame.
sentence.content_zh: 男：现在我认识的人几乎没有谁能认出我的笔迹了。真有点可惜。

sentence.content: WOMAN: I know. I feel the same way. I used to write a diary by hand and now I do that digitally. It just seems less effort to do it that way. So it’s not just a problem…
sentence.content_zh: 女：我也有同感。我以前还坚持用手写日记，现在改用电子方式了。感觉这样轻松多了。所以这不只是个人的问题……

added_content.ex_information:

TWO
letters,
A–E
.

obj.content: For children with dyspraxia, which [b] TWO [/b] problems with handwriting do the students think are easiest to correct?
obj.html_content: For children with dyspraxia, which TWO problems with handwriting do the students think are easiest to correct?

obj.content: not spacing letters correctly
obj.html_content: not spacing letters correctly

obj.content: not writing in a straight line
obj.html_content: not writing in a straight line

obj.content: applying too much pressure when writing
obj.html_content: applying too much pressure when writing

obj.content: confusing letter shapes
obj.html_content: confusing letter shapes

obj.content: What does the woman say about using laptops to teach writing to children with dyslexia?
obj.html_content: What does the woman say about using laptops to teach writing to children with dyslexia?

obj.content: Children often lack motivation to learn that way.
obj.html_content: Children often lack motivation to learn that way.

obj.html_content: Children become fluent relatively quickly.

obj.content: The woman is concerned that relying on digital devices has made it difficult for her to
obj.html_content: The woman is concerned that relying on digital devices has made it difficult for her to

obj.content: take detailed notes.
obj.html_content: take detailed notes.

obj.content: spell and punctuate.
obj.html_content: spell and punctuate.

obj.content: read old documents.
obj.html_content: read old documents.

obj.content: How do the students feel about their own handwriting?
obj.html_content: How do the students feel about their own handwriting?

obj.content: concerned they are unable to write quickly
obj.html_content: concerned they are unable to write quickly

obj.content: embarrassed by comments made about it
obj.html_content: embarrassed by comments made about it

obj.content: regretful that they have lost the habit
obj.html_content: regretful that they have lost the habit

sentence.content: MAN: How are you getting on with the assignment on handwriting?
sentence.content_zh: 男：你那篇关于书写能力的作业做得怎么样了？

sentence.content: WOMAN: Not too bad. You know, I hadn’t realised that children benefit in so many ways from learning to write.
sentence.content_zh: 女：还不错。你知道吗，我以前不知道孩子们从学习书写中能获得这么多好处。

sentence.content: WOMAN: It’s such an important skill and yet most people think handwriting is less important than in the past because people hardly ever write by hand these days.
sentence.content_zh: 女：这是一项非常重要的技能，但大多数人认为现在书写已经没有以前那么重要了，因为现在几乎没人用手写字了。

sentence.content: MAN: Yes. And all the evidence suggests children should learn to write by hand before they learn to type. Not least because it helps their memory.
sentence.content_zh: 男：是的。而且所有证据都表明，孩子应该先学会手写，再学打字。其中一个主要原因就是这对他们的记忆力有帮助。

sentence.content: WOMAN: That’s right. The physical act of writing helps children to remember letters. That seems pretty obvious when you think about it.
sentence.content_zh: 女：没错。动手写字这个行为本身就能帮助孩子记住字母的形状。仔细想想其实挺明显的。

sentence.content: MAN: What’s less obvious is how it helps develop their concentration. They have to sit still and focus on one thing.
sentence.content_zh: 男：不太明显的是它如何帮助他们发展注意力。他们必须坐好，专注于一件事上。

sentence.content: WOMAN: Yeah. That aspect of handwriting had never occurred to me before.
sentence.content_zh: 女：对。这方面我以前真的没怎么想过。

sentence.content: MAN: Same here. I’m not sure I understand how it improves children’s imagination though.
sentence.content_zh: 男：我也是。不过我不太明白它是怎么提升孩子想象力的。

sentence.content: WOMAN: Well, there was that study which showed that primary aged children generated more ideas when they were writing by hand than using a keyboard. I would’ve guessed that would be the case.
sentence.content_zh: 女：嗯，有一项研究显示，小学生在手写时比用键盘能产生更多创意点子。我觉得这也很合理。

sentence.content: MAN: Mmm, yeah. I’d never associated spatial awareness with handwriting either. I thought spatial awareness was more to do with knowing where you are in relation to objects or other people.
sentence.content_zh: 男：嗯，是的。我也从来没把空间意识和手写联系在一起过。我一直以为空间意识是指你对自己在物体或他人周围位置的判断。

sentence.content: WOMAN: I thought that too. But good spatial awareness is essential for writing because you have to space words correctly.
sentence.content_zh: 女：我以前也这么想。但良好的空间意识对手写来说其实非常重要，因为你得正确地安排单词之间的间距。

sentence.content: WOMAN: It’s not just fine motor skills that improve through writing as I’d always assumed.
sentence.content_zh: 女：原来我以为只是精细动作技能会通过书写得到提高。

sentence.content: MAN: Handwriting is so much harder for children with dyspraxia, who have problems coordinating movement.
sentence.content_zh: 男：对于患有动作协调障碍的孩子来说，书写要困难得多，因为他们很难控制身体动作。

sentence.content: MAN: It’s good there are lots of things you can do in the classroom to help them.
sentence.content_zh: 男：幸好有很多方法可以在教室里帮助他们。

sentence.content: MAN: They need so much more support with letter formation – you need to play lots of games to help them distinguish letter shapes. It takes a lot of patience.
sentence.content_zh: 男：他们在形成字母方面需要更多的支持——你需要玩很多游戏来帮助他们区分不同字母的形状。这很考验耐心。

sentence.content: WOMAN: Yeah. I liked the idea of using one of those pens that lights up if you press too hard. That seems like a really simple solution.
sentence.content_zh: 女：嗯。我喜欢那个如果用力太大会亮灯的笔的设计，看起来是个很简单有效的办法。

sentence.content: MAN: Yes, absolutely. I’m not sure there’s much you can do about children with dyspraxia writing very slowly.
sentence.content_zh: 男：是的，确实如此。不过我也不确定有没有办法让患有动作协调障碍的孩子写得更快一些。

sentence.content: MAN: It’s more important to focus on accuracy and as they get more confident I think they eventually speed up.
sentence.content_zh: 男：更重要的是准确性，随着他们越来越自信，速度也会慢慢提上来。

sentence.content: WOMAN: One quite simple thing you can do is to use grid paper. So they write each letter in a box and that trains them to space the letters correctly.
sentence.content_zh: 女：有个比较简单的方法是使用格子纸，每个字母写在一个格子里，这样可以训练他们正确安排字母间距。

sentence.content: MAN: Indeed, that’s more important for legibility than trying to get them to write in a straight line.
sentence.content_zh: 男：没错，比起让他们写出一条直线，清晰的字母间距对辨识度更为重要。

sentence.content: MAN: For some children it might be better to teach them to write on a laptop rather than by hand. Like children with dyslexia.
sentence.content_zh: 男：有些孩子可能更适合教他们在笔记本电脑上打字而不是手写，比如像患有诵读困难症的孩子。

sentence.content: MAN: They often really struggle with handwriting and some just give up.
sentence.content_zh: 男：他们经常在书写方面遇到很大困难，有些人甚至直接放弃了。

sentence.content: WOMAN: Yeah. It’s not as frustrating for them if they get things wrong – on a keyboard they can be more willing to have a go. But I read that developing fluency isn’t any faster.
sentence.content_zh: 女：是的。对他们来说，写错也没关系，用键盘更容易让他们愿意尝试。但我读到说，这样在提高写作流利度方面并没有更快。

sentence.content: MAN: That’s right. Did you read that article on the benefits of teaching print rather than cursive handwriting, where the letters are joined up?
sentence.content_zh: 男：没错。你有没有看过那篇关于教印刷体而非连笔草书的好处的文章？

sentence.content: WOMAN: Yes. Well in the past cursive writing was certainly considered more stylish and educated.
sentence.content_zh: 女：看了。过去人们普遍认为连笔书法更优雅、更有文化感。

sentence.content: WOMAN: But not anymore. Teachers’ attitudes have changed because it’s been proved that cursive is more difficult to learn – especially for children with learning difficulties who find joining up letters really challenging.
sentence.content_zh: 女：但现在不是了。教师们的看法已经改变，因为事实证明连笔更难学——特别是对那些学习困难的孩子，他们觉得把字母连起来特别难。

sentence.content: MAN: I agree. I was always worried that my poor handwriting affected my exam results. And now research shows that I was right to worry.
sentence.content_zh: 男：我同意这点。我一直担心我的糟糕字迹会影响考试成绩，现在研究证明我当时担心是有道理的。

sentence.content: MAN: I’m sure a lot of students think it’s unfair that they’re being judged on their handwriting not just their knowledge.
sentence.content_zh: 男：我相信很多学生会觉得不公平，因为评判他们不仅看知识掌握程度，还要看字写得好不好。

sentence.content: WOMAN: Marks are definitely affected if examiners can’t read the script. That’s why it’s always been so important to teach children to write legibly….
sentence.content_zh: 女：如果阅卷老师看不懂字迹，分数肯定会受影响。这就是为什么一直强调教会孩子写清楚的重要性……

sentence.content: WOMAN: Do you think the role of handwriting will change in the future?
sentence.content_zh: 女：你觉得将来书写的角色会改变吗？

sentence.content: MAN: I can’t see that changing much. Touch typing still isn’t taught in most schools, which is a shame. But maybe that won’t be necessary in the future because people will also be able to write by hand on digital devices.
sentence.content_zh: 男：我看不会有多大变化。毕竟大多数学校仍然不教盲打，这挺可惜的。但也许未来这并不重要，因为人们也可以在数字设备上用手写输入了。

sentence.content: MAN: Anyway, teachers understand the value of handwriting. It’s a basic life skill.
sentence.content_zh: 男：不管怎么说，老师们还是知道书写的价值的。它是一种基本的生活技能。

sentence.content: WOMAN: True. However, the fact is that people are writing by hand less and less and relying on digital devices. That does cause some problems.
sentence.content_zh: 女：没错。但现实是，人们越来越少用手写字，而是依赖数码设备。这也带来了一些问题。

sentence.content: MAN: You mean like note taking? There are lots of apps for that.
sentence.content_zh: 男：你是说像记笔记这种事吗？这类应用现在已经有很多了。

sentence.content: WOMAN: And for reading historical documents apparently. But my mum is shocked by my awful spelling and the fact that my punctuation is really inconsistent. I think you can put that down to lack of practice.
sentence.content_zh: 女：还有阅读历史文献的时候据说也有问题。但我妈妈总抱怨我拼写差得离谱，标点也非常混乱。我觉得这是因为缺乏练习。

sentence.content: MAN: I expect so. Personally, I miss writing by hand. I hardly ever write anything now. I remember my grandparents had such beautiful handwriting and it was so individual.
sentence.content_zh: 男：应该是这样的。我自己倒是怀念写字的感觉。我现在几乎什么都不写了。我还记得我祖父母那优美的字迹，每个人都有自己的风格。

sentence.content: MAN: Nobody I know would be able to identify my handwriting now. It’s a shame.
sentence.content_zh: 男：现在我认识的人几乎没有谁能认出我的笔迹了。真有点可惜。

sentence.content: WOMAN: I know. I feel the same way. I used to write a diary by hand and now I do that digitally. It just seems less effort to do it that way. So it’s not just a problem…
sentence.content_zh: 女：我也有同感。我以前还坚持用手写日记，现在改用电子方式了。感觉这样轻松多了。所以这不只是个人的问题……

officialExplain.explain:
这部分题目中需要注意选项相关信息是否符合题干问题中的both students和surprising。原文中的hadn’t realised对应题干问题中的surprising，children原词重现，原文的动词benefit对应题干的名词benefits。

B：原文中男士提及helps their memory，对应B选项，但后续内容中女士讲到pretty obvious，与题干问题中的surprising不符，排除B。

C：之后男士讲到的less obvious对应题干问题中的surprising，相关信息为helps develop their concentration，对应C选项；女士讲到以前从未想到，never occurred to me before，其中occur to表示“（观念或想法）被想到”，男士回应Same here，两人的表述表现出surprising。因此C为正确选项之一。

D：原文中男士提及不确定自己是否理解如何improves children’s imagination，女士进一步解释有研究表明generated more ideas…，之后男士表示了认同。这些虽然对应D选项，但未体现题干问题中的both students和surprising，排除D。

E：之后男士讲到never associated spatial awareness with handwriting，对应E选项，space awareness表示“空间意识”。男士说的never associated以及后续解释说明中的I thought…，女士回应的I thought that too以及紧接着讲到But…，这些体现出原以为的情况不同，对应题干问题中的both students和surprising。因此E为正确选项之一。

A：女士讲到良好的空间意识对于书写的重要性之后，紧接着讲到不仅fine motor skills能够通过书写提升，对应A选项，fine motor skills表示“精细运动技能”。但同一句中的I’d always assumed与题干问题中的surprising不符，排除A。

因此21~22题答案为CE。

这部分题目需要注意选项相关信息是否符合题干中的easiest to correct。在原文中children with dyspraxia和problems都原词重现，dyspraxia意为“运用障碍”。原文中coordinating对应的动词原形是coordinate，意为“使（身体部位）动作协调”。

D：原文中男士讲到的letter formation和letter shapes，对应D选项。但相关信息中，need so much more support、lots of games以及a lot of patience，都表现出并不容易，不符合题干问题中的easiest，排除D。

C：原文中女士讲到pens和press too hard，对应C选项。后续表述really simple solution对应题干问题中的easiest to correct，男士回应Yes以及absolutely。因此C为正确选项之一。

E：原文男士所述内容中，very slowly原词重现，对应E选项。但相关表述是not sure there’s much you can do，加上后续说到关注准确度更重要，不符合题干问题中的easiest to correct，排除E。

A：原文中女士讲到One quite simple thing对应题干问题中的easiest to correct。后续讲到trains them to space the letters correctly，对应A选项，男士回应Indeed。因此A为正确选项之一。

B：原文中男士所述内容中，in a straight line原词重现，对应B选项。但相关表述是more important for legibility than…，表达了相比起来清晰可读是更重要的，不符合题干问题中的easiest to correct，排除B。

因此23~24题答案为AC。

本题题干中问的是What does the woman say about，需要注意正确选项与对话中女士所述内容有关。原文对话中，男士所述内容里先讲到laptop，对应题干的laptops，原文的teach them to write对应题干的teach writing，children with dyslexia原词重现。接下来提及struggle以及give up，与A选项的lack motivation有关联，但与题干问的人物woman不符，排除A。女士说到not as frustrating和more willing to have a go对应C选项的react more positively，原文的get things wrong对应选项的make a mistake。其中willing意为“乐意，愿意”，have a go表示“尝试，试图”。之后女士还提及fluency，对应B选项的fluent，但原文的isn’t any faster与选项中relatively quickly不符，排除B。因此本题答案为C。

结合题干中的woman，本题仍需注意正确选项与对话中女士所述内容有关。原文对话中，男士讲到print和cursive handwriting，对应题干中的cursive和print writing。后续内容中，女士先讲到in the past，过去的情况是，cursive writing被认为more stylish and educated，这里与B选项academic performance有关。原文以cursive writing为主语，B选项以print writing为主语，在对两者的比较中体现原文的more…和选项的lower…。但原文的past和后续讲到的not anymore，反映出与现在情况不符，排除B。女士继续讲到cursive和more difficult to learn，对应A选项中的cursive writing和disadvantages，这里的disadvantage用作动词，表示“使处于不利地位”；原文的children with learning difficulties对应选项的certain group of children。因此本题答案为A。

原文中poor handwriting和exam原词重现，对应本题题干。题干问题中问的是impact，表示“影响”，原文的affected对应题干的impact，A选项也包括affected。原文的exam results对应题干exam performance，也对应A中的grades。紧接着原文中说到research shows对应A选项的evidence。后续原文中so important对应B选项，原文的legibly对应选项的Neat，legibly是legible的副词形式，可以理解为“清晰可读地”，neat意为“整齐的，整洁的”，但这部分原文讲到的always been so important，与选项中和过去对比less important这一表述不符，排除B。因此本题答案为A。

结合题干中的man，需要注意正确选项与对话中男士所述内容有关。原文中future原词重现，对应本题题干。女士问的具体内容是the role of handwriting未来是否将发生变化，handwriting为这部分整体讨论的主题，也同时对应本题不同选项中的writing/write by hand。男士的回应I can’t see that changing much，没有很大变化表现出B选项的continue to learn。接下来男士提及Touch typing，与A选项有关，但所述原文中isn’t taught和maybe that won’t be necessary in the future与选项不符，排除A。因此本题答案为B。

结合题干中的woman，需要注意正确选项与对话中女士所述内容有关。女士讲到relying on digital devices，对应本题题干原词，原文的That does cause some problems对应题干的concerned。后续原文中，男士提及note taking对应A选项，女士提及reading historical documents对应C选项，但这些未体现题干中的concerned或difficult，排除C。接下来在But之后，女士讲到spelling和punctuation，分别是B选项中动词spell 和punctuate对应的名词形式；原文的my awful…和inconsistent对应题干的make it difficult for her to…。因此本题答案为B。
关于自己的书写，原文中男士讲到的miss和hardly ever write anything now，对应C选项的have lost the habit。后续表示It’s a shame，a shame在这里表示“令人惋惜或遗憾的事”，对应C中的regretful，意为“后悔的，失望的”。女士的回应中，I know和feel the same way表达了一致的感受。因此本题答案为C。
